title: Inserting a row
prompt_md: Add a new user to the `users` table with `id` `4`, `name` `Dev`, and `email` `dev@example.com`. Use `RETURNING id, name` so the inserted row comes back.
starter_code: INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (4, 'Dev', 'dev@example.com') RETURNING id, name;
hints: `INSERT INTO table (columns...) VALUES (...)` adds a row.
In Postgres, append `RETURNING col1, col2` to get the new row back as a result set.
reference_solution: INSERT INTO users (id, name, email) VALUES (4, 'Dev', 'dev@example.com') RETURNING id, name;
